  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) content-level assessment (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment) or (B) sentence-level writing polish and clarity?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A core part of reviewing this section is checking whether the stated mechanisms and conclusions about scaling logically follow from the premises provided in the text.",
    "R1.3": "The review should verify that each claim about commitment behavior across fleet sizes is actually supported by what is reported in the scaling experiment descriptions.",
    "R1.6": "Reviewing typically includes assessing sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, conciseness, and readability to ensure the section communicates effectively.",
    "RX.1": "The review should explicitly note uncertainties or limits where the text does not fully substantiate a claim.",
    "RX.2": "The review should guard against overinterpreting the section or inferring results beyond what the text states.",
    "RX.3": "The review must remain scoped to the provided section and not rely on external context or missing figures.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should be framed to avoid undue negative impact or mischaracterization of the work’s claims.",
    "RX.5": "Any review points should be traceable to specific sentences or passages in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) content-level assessment (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment) or (B) sentence-level writing polish and clarity?

rationale.R1.2: A core part of reviewing this section is checking whether the stated mechanisms and conclusions about scaling logically follow from the premises provided in the text.
rationale.R1.3: The review should verify that each claim about commitment behavior across fleet sizes is actually supported by what is reported in the scaling experiment descriptions.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing typically includes assessing sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, conciseness, and readability to ensure the section communicates effectively.
rationale.RX.1: The review should explicitly note uncertainties or limits where the text does not fully substantiate a claim.
rationale.RX.2: The review should guard against overinterpreting the section or inferring results beyond what the text states.
rationale.RX.3: The review must remain scoped to the provided section and not rely on external context or missing figures.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should be framed to avoid undue negative impact or mischaracterization of the work’s claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any review points should be traceable to specific sentences or passages in the provided text.
